title: paspalum petilum
intro: Paspalum petilum, also known as 'Cotton-Grass', is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Paspalum petilum include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Paspalum petilum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions.
